description: To direct one's will toward a future action or outcome; purposeful mental aiming. 'Thueli' names the arrow of will—consciousness pointing toward what it seeks to bring about.
sound_symbolism: The word aims thoughtfully. 'Thu' breathes with deliberate opening, will gathering. 'E' eases into direction, finding the path. Then 'li' lifts toward the aimed-for goal.
grammatical_notes: Functions as verb: 'mi thueli' (I intend). Takes object of intention. Related to 'porua' (purpose) as the act behind the aim.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Right intention—the second factor of the path, directing mind toward wholesome ends.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Declared intention—speaking what one aims for, enabling trust and cooperation.
